methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $300M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Mara is a pre-seed stage company in Defense & Security founded by Daniel Kofman and Sriram Raghu. The broader sector includes 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables for reliable median valuation anchoring.

Given data constraints, a stage-progression method applies. Using $300M as a raised-capital proxy baseline and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for pre-seed advancement yields $750M. This reflects the step-up valuation premium expected when transitioning from pre-seed to the next funding round.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $638M to $862M, with a median of $750M. This range carries material uncertainty: it derives from a single applicable method rather than triangulation across multiple approaches. The absence of narrow-stage peer comparables limits confidence in external benchmarking. Key variables affecting final valuation include product-market validation, customer traction post-$1.6M seed raise, and defensibility within an increasingly crowded defense-tech landscape. Further refinement would require evidence of revenue, customer commitments, or technical differentiation relative to the identified peer set.
